perturbation.original_text: 2.1 Licensor hereby grants Licensed Users a nonexclusive, nontransferable, nonsublicensable, royalty-free license, during the term of this Agreement, to use and display the Licensed Trade Name and the Licensed Mark in the United States solely in connection with the Licensee's corporate name and identifying mark.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 Licensor hereby grants Licensed Users an exclusive, transferable, sublicensable, royalty-bearing license, during the initial five years of this Agreement, to use and display the Licensed Trade Name and the Licensed Mark in the United States solely in connection with the Licensee's corporate name and identifying mark.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the license from 'nonexclusive' to 'exclusive' and 'nontransferable, nonsublicensable, royalty-free' to 'transferable, sublicensable, royalty-bearing' and introducing a time restriction 'initial five years', we create a direct contradiction with Section 2.3, which reserves all rights, including granting others the right to use the Licensed Mark, to the Licensor. Also introducing time restriction contradicts 3.1 and 3.2 where there is no any time limitation except termination upon certain conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All of Licensor's rights in and to the Licensed Mark, including, but not limited to, the right to use and to grant others the right to use the Licensed Mark, are reserved by Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted a nonexclusive, nontransferable, nonsublicensable, royalty-free license. The changed text grants an exclusive, transferable, sublicensable, royalty-bearing license for the initial five years. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Licensor reserves all rights, including the right to grant others the right to use the Licensed Mark. The change makes the license exclusive, meaning the licensor can't grant others the same right during the first five years, directly conflicting with the Licensor's retained rights. The introduction of a limited term also introduces a contradiction as it implies limitations on usage not present in other sections. The shift from non-transferable to transferable and non-sublicensable to sublicensable further exacerbates this contradiction because it modifies the rights originally retained by the licensor.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving the other Party thirty (30) days' prior written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Either Party may terminate this Agreement only for a material breach, and such termination requires one hundred and eighty (180) days' prior written notice, and is subject to arbitration.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows either party to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice. The modified clause restricts termination to cases of material breach, increases the notice period to 180 days, and introduces mandatory arbitration, contradicting the freedom to terminate outlined in the original text. This change directly contradicts 4.10, which specifies governing law and jurisdiction, implying court action is possible without mandatory arbitration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The provisions of this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California (excluding any conflict of law rule or principle that would refer to the laws of another jurisdiction). Each Party hereto irrevocably submits to the jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in California, in any action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement, and each Party hereby irrevocably agrees that all claims in respect of any such action or proceeding must be brought and/or defended in any such court; provided, however, that matters which are under the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal courts shall be brought in the Federal District Court for the Central District of California. Each Party hereto consents to service of process by any means authorized by the applicable law of the forum in any action brought under or arising out of this Agreement, and each Party irrevocably waives, to the fullest extent each may effectively do so, the defense of an inconvenient forum to the maintenance of such action or proceeding in any such court.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 3.1 introduces mandatory arbitration and restricts termination to material breaches, contradicting the original clause allowing termination with 30 days' notice. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which grants jurisdiction to state and federal courts in California for any action or proceeding related to the agreement, implying that court action is possible without mandatory arbitration, which is now contradicted with the new version of clause 3.1.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 LIMITATION OF WARRANTY AND LIABILITY. LICENSOR DOES NOT MAKE WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, RELATED TO OR ARISING OUT OF THE LICENSED MARK OR THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 EXTENDED WARRANTY AND LIABILITY. LICENSOR HEREBY PROVIDES WARRANTIES OF ALL KIND, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, RELATED TO OR ARISING OUT OF THE LICENSED MARK OR THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states that the Licensor provides no warranties. The modified clause states the opposite: the Licensor provides all kinds of warranties. This directly contradicts 4.2.1, which disclaims specific implied warranties. By adding an extended warranty the agreement is being unclear and creating the In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LICENSOR SPECIFICALLY DISCLAIMS ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, NON-INFRINGEMENT AND TITLE, AND ALL OTHER WARRANTIES THAT MAY OTHERWISE ARISE FROM COURSE OF DEALING, USAGE OF TRADE OR CUSTOM.
perturbation.justification: The original text in 4.2 explicitly states that the Licensor provides no warranties. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the Licensor provides all kinds of warranties. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which specifically disclaims any implied warranties, because now, according to 4.2, all kinds of warranties are provided.
